revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大将軍神社 (京都市東山区)]]
*
{{Infobox religious building |名称 = {{ill|Daishōgun Shrine|ja|大将軍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishogun Shrine|qq=}} |image = File:Taishōgun Shrine, Higashiyama ward, Kyoto - May 2, 2014.jpg |所在地 = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}{{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama Ward|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}}{{ill|Sanjō Ōhashi|en|Sanjō Ōhashi|qid=Q3087620|lt=Sanjo Ohashi|1=Sanjō Ōhashi}}, Higashi 3-chōme, Shitahiru Nagamitsu-chō 640 |緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 00 |緯度秒 = 32.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 |経度秒 = 36.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図 = Japan Kyoto city2 |ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |神体 = |社格 = [[旧村社]] |創建 = 延暦13rd year |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Higashiyama-ku|qid=Q1203862|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.00913888888889|N|135.77691666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Taishogun Shrine''' ({{ill|Taishogun Jinja|ja|大将軍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishogun Jinja|1=Daishōgun Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11434525}}) is a Shinto shrine located in Higashiyama Ward of Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. == History and Tradition == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} as its principal deity and also enshrines {{ill|Fujiwara no Kaneie|en|Fujiwara no Kaneie|qid=Q1321703|lt=Fujiwara Kaneie|1=Fujiwara no Kaneie}}. In 794 (the 13th year of the Enryaku era), during the construction of {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|qid=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō|1=Heian-kyō}}, Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|qid=Q314846|lt=Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}} established a series of shrines around the capital to protect the {{ill|Heian Palace|en|Heian Palace|qid=Q304501|lt=Heian Palace|1=Heian Palace}}. Taishogun Shrine was one of these, sited at the southeastern corner of the capital. In particular, this location corresponds to one of “Kyō no Shichikuchi” (the seven exits of Kyoto), specifically the {{ill|Sanjo-guchi|ja|三条口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjo-guchi|1=Sanjo-guchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q23999052}}, and was regarded as strategically important to prevent the incursion of malevolent spirits. Although Fujiwara Kaneie’s residence, known as the Higashi Sanjo-dono, once stood in the area, it was ruined during the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|qid=Q385676|lt=Onin War|1=Ōnin War}}; today, its legacy survives in the form of the Higashi Sanjo-sha that remains within the shrine precincts.<ref>[https://kanko.city.kyoto.lg.jp/detail.php?InforKindCode=1&ManageCode=1000396 Taishogun Shrine] – Kyoto Official Tourism Site, Kyoto Tourism Navi</ref> == Surroundings == * [[Dan'ō Hōrin-ji]] == Access == * [[Sanjo Station (Kyoto Prefecture)|Sanjo Station]] * [[:en:Sanjo Keihan Station|Sanjo Keihan Station]] == Footnotes == {{reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Twitter|qq=}} {{pref-stub-京都府|qq=}} {{shinto-stub|qq=}}
